description_long: This is a tall, narrow stone tower with a conical red-tiled roof topped by a red flag. The tower's body is constructed from gray stone blocks, giving it a robust, medieval appearance. The upper section features crenellations, reminiscent of defensive battlements, and small slit-like openings that may serve as arrow loops or windows. The base of the tower has a prominent arched wooden doorway, suggesting an entry point for access. The roof is steeply pitched and finished with a decorative finial at the peak, from which the flag waves. The overall design is evocative of a watchtower or lookout post commonly found in fortified towns or castles.
description: A tall, medieval-style stone tower with a red-tiled conical roof, battlements, and a waving red flag. It features a prominent arched wooden doorway at the base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the arched wooden doorway, battlements, and the red flag extending to the right.
description_view_1: Side view with the flag visible but no doorway, highlighting the stone texture and roof tiles.
description_view_2: Rear view with battlements and flag extending left, no visible entrance.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, showing the stonework and roof, with the flag mostly hidden.
